cases.name: P2PMarket
cases.comment: Benchmarks the LocalDOT P2PMarket contract (https://github.com/paritytech/p2p-market). P2P cash-to-PAS exchange with handoff agents and native-token escrow. Each day uses a dedicated PROVIDER_D who self-registers as their own agent — all of that day's trade-lifecycle txs (lockTrade → confirmCashReceived → confirmPickup) come from one sender so mempool nonce ordering serializes the within-trade dependencies. Across days, 5 distinct senders run concurrently. Models 5 days × 100 trades/day. Hot path: lockTrade (Trade struct write + escrow value transfer + userTrades array pushes on 3 addresses) + confirmCashReceived (state transition + value transfer to buyer) + confirmPickup (final state transition).

inputs.comment: Allocate OWNER. Just deploys the contract; nothing privileged afterward.
Deploy P2PMarket. Constructor seeds tokenPricePerCurrency for USD and EUR.

Per-day setup repeat: 5 iters. For each day D we allocate PROVIDER_D, register them as a handoff agent, then have them create an offer where they themselves are the only agent in agentAddresses. consolidate_state propagates PROVIDER_0..N-1 and OFFER_ID_0..N-1 to the case scope. start_watcher: true ⇒ the watcher cutoff is the block right after the warm-up iter (D=0) finishes; the other setup parallel iters and the entire measured days loop below are tracked.

steps.comment: PROVIDER_K self-registers as a handoff agent. metadataCID and the rest are static across iters — the contract only stores them verbatim.
PROVIDER_K creates a SELL offer listing themselves as the only allowed agent. The returned offerId is captured into OFFER_ID_K so the measured trades below can reference it.

inputs.comment: Days outer: 5 days. D=0 warm-up runs its inner trades sequentially; D=1..4 are parallel outer iters, each running the inner trades loop from a distinct PROVIDER_D. Net chain-level parallelism: N_DAYS independent sender nonce streams.

steps.comment: Trades inner: 100 parallel iters per day. Each iter does the full 3-step agent-mediated trade lifecycle: lockTrade (provider locks tokens), confirmCashReceived (agent = same provider confirms cash from buyer ⇒ tokens released to buyer), confirmPickup (provider confirms they received the cash off-chain ⇒ trade COMPLETED). All 3 txs from PROVIDER_D in nonce order ⇒ mempool guarantees correct execution order and the lockTrade's returned tradeId resolves before confirmCashReceived's dry-run.
